mitigation.title: Enable sensitive log redaction
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by updating OpenClaw log redaction settings. After remediation, sensitive tool and runtime data in logs will be masked.

form_schema.label: Enable automatic redaction for tool logs

mitigation.title: Enable gateway authentication
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by configuring an authentication mode and credentials. Enabling gateway authentication blocks unauthorized access.

mitigation.title: Migrate from password authentication
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by switching to token mode or rotating credentials. Token mode is recommended because it reduces weak password exposure.

mitigation.title: Fix config file permissions
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by tightening config file permissions. The remediation limits who can read sensitive configuration values.

form_schema.label: Fix file permissions using ACL or chmod 600

mitigation.title: Fix config directory permissions
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by tightening config directory permissions. The remediation restricts access to sensitive files stored in the directory.

form_schema.label: Fix directory permissions using ACL or chmod 700

mitigation.title: Restrict gateway bind address
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by rebinding the gateway to a loopback address. The remediation reduces external network exposure.

mitigation.title: Replace weak password protection
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by switching to token authentication or setting a stronger password. Token mode is the safer default.

mitigation.title: Rotate weak token
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by generating a stronger token and replacing the old one. The remediation lowers credential guessing risk.

mitigation.title: Enable default sandbox mode
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by enabling a supported sandbox mode. Sandbox isolation reduces the impact of risky actions on the host system.

mitigation.title: Enable agent sandboxing
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by enabling sandboxing for the agent execution environment. Use an available isolation backend to reduce filesystem and command risks.

mitigation.title: Fix log directory permissions
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by tightening log directory permissions. The remediation reduces unauthorized read and tamper access to log files.

form_schema.label: Fix directory permissions using ACL or chmod 700

mitigation.title: Remove plaintext secrets
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by moving secrets to environment variables or by selecting a manual review path. Removing plaintext secrets reduces credential leakage risk.

mitigation.title: Scan unreviewed skills
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by running the skill security scan. The remediation fills in missing risk results for installed skills.

mitigation.title: Remediate risky skill
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by applying a skill remediation action. If the skill is not trusted, delete it or disable it.

mitigation.title: Manually remediate OpenClaw one click RCE
mitigation.description: This risk requires manual remediation and should only present operational guidance. Prioritize upgrade, entry point hardening, and runtime controls.

items.action: Check the current version and upgrade
items.detail: Run the version command and upgrade to a fixed OpenClaw release as soon as possible.
Review UI and startup flows and remove URL parameter paths that can override gatewayUrl.
Enforce an allowlist for Origin during gateway connection authorization.
Avoid long lived plaintext browser storage for sensitive tokens and move to safer storage controls.
Apply CSP, frame protections, and transport security headers to the management UI and gateway endpoints.
Restrict the system so it can only connect to approved gateway URLs.
Generate a visible warning when gatewayUrl or related high risk settings change.
Add version review, code review, and targeted security verification to the release process.
Monitor unusual gateway connections, unusual origins, and suspicious credential usage patterns.

items.action: Train teams on secure gateway design
items.detail: Document and train around URL trust boundaries, credential management, and gateway authorization design.
